feedback.text: Hello! MM here again. I have forgotten about a matter that I find very important. It is about the payment processors like PayPal and Visa, these companies have recently pressured game distributers like itch.io and Steam to remove games that have sexual content in them (Very important note: Legal NSFW games.) to be removed and with this pressure they managed to remove 27 thousand games. These games have dissapeared from users their library without any notice nor refund. This is very concerning as it boils down to the middle man [the payment processors] are deciding what you can and cannot buy with YOUR money! It is absolutely outragous and I am asking for a act or law to be added to protect the consumers and the game distributing platforms from this, I find it is infracting on our freedom since the payment processors are limiting what we're allowed to consume. They got fully legal content removed from distributers and the people their library who bought said content since the company behind the payment processors does not approve of said items. This should not happen, they should only interfere when the content that is being attempted to be purchased / available via their payment processor is illegal by law, and they shouldn't be able to interfere with purchases / content availability if it is not abiding by their company policy / values. They should not be able to have such influence on what can and can't be sold on other distributers their platforms based on the company's (the payment processors) their morals and policy's. They are there to make online payment possible and that should be it! They should not be able to decide what content can be up on other distributers their sites / services. [Big opinion here.] If they don't like what is being sold on said distributer their market / sit the processor should refuse the ability to pay for those games with only their service (And not use any other service or any other company or way to remove, rid or limit said content) or just leave, NOT flat out remove the content that they don't even own / are distributing purely since they don't like said legal content available on the platform. Thanks for reading my feedback and listening to my concerns, I am wishing a good day to any person reading this. -MM
